strategies.name: Scalar VF interleave stress (IC=4)
strategies.target: Mutate llvm/test/Transforms/LoopVectorize/conditional-scalar-assignment-interleave-only.ll to force -force-vector-width=1 -force-vector-interleave=4 and shape the loop so only a late part (e.g. part3) updates the scalar reduction.
strategies.rationale: The patch’s core change is making ExtractLastActive variadic and appending per-part (data,mask) pairs during UF unroll. Existing coverage is mostly IC=2. IC=4 is the first meaningful stress for the new operand-growth logic and select-chain ordering in scalar VF mode.
strategies.expected_issue: Middle-block reduction value uses only part0/part1 (or wrong precedence), producing incorrect live-out when the last matching iteration is in part3.

strategies.name: Odd interleave factor ordering check (IC=3)
strategies.target: Mutate the same interleave-only find-last test to use -force-vector-interleave=3 and craft masks so multiple parts are active with known precedence (must choose the latest active part).
strategies.rationale: Patch claims support for arbitrary interleave factors. Odd UF can expose hidden assumptions in loops that were previously only exercised with UF=2/power-of-two.
strategies.expected_issue: Incorrect result due to wrong concatenation order across parts (e.g., part1 overriding part2/part3 unexpectedly), especially with non-power-of-two/odd UF.

strategies.name: Vector VF chained ExtractLastActive (IC=4)
strategies.target: Mutate llvm/test/Transforms/LoopVectorize/find-last.ll (generic find-last, not FindLastIV) to force -force-vector-width=4 -force-vector-interleave=4 and FileCheck for multi-stage extract-last-active chaining in middle.block.
strategies.rationale: In vector mode, generate() now emits iterative llvm.experimental.vector.extract.last.active calls with previous result as default. This is new behavior and should be explicitly checked for UF>2.
strategies.expected_issue: Generated vector middle.block does not contain chained extract-last-active accumulation across all parts (missing calls or wrong default threading), leading to wrong final scalar extraction.

strategies.name: Remainder/epilogue interaction with interleaved find-last
strategies.target: Mutate a find-last interleave test to use runtime trip count (non-constant, non-multiple of VF*IC) under forced IC>2 and check the BC_MERGE_RDX / LCSSA path selects vector middle result correctly before scalar cleanup.
strategies.rationale: Even if per-part aggregation is correct, the live-out can still be wrong at the vector-to-scalar handoff when a scalar remainder loop exists. This is a high-risk edge for conditional scalar assignment patterns.
strategies.expected_issue: Incorrect merge between vector middle-block result and scalar epilogue result when N is not divisible by VF*UF; last active element may be taken from wrong region.

strategies.name: VPlan verifier crash guard for null-underlying widened PHI
strategies.target: Run mutated conditional-scalar-assignment interleave test with -vplan-verify-each and UF>1 (prefer UF=4) and check pass stability (no verifier/assert failure).
strategies.rationale: Patch changed VPWidenPHIRecipe::clone from cast<PHINode> to cast_if_present<PHINode>, indicating prior crash potential. This should be directly exercised with -vplan-verify-each on an interleaved conditional assignment case that creates synthetic mask/data widened phis.
strategies.expected_issue: Verifier/assert/crash during VPlan unroll/clone for synthetic VPWidenPHIRecipe with null underlying PHI (regression if cast_if_present path is incomplete).

reason_thou:
1. Fix Understanding
- The fix enables LoopVectorize interleaving for find-last/conditional scalar assignment patterns beyond the previously blocked configuration.
- Key semantic changes:
  - Removed a late processLoop() guard that unconditionally disabled interleaving when a FindLast reduction existed.
  - Re-defined VPInstruction::ExtractLastActive from fixed 3-operand semantics to variadic semantics: operand0 is scalar default, followed by repeated (data, mask) pairs.
  - Updated VPlanTransforms::handleFindLastReductions() to build ExtractLastActive with new operand order: {default, data, mask}.
  - Updated unroll logic to append extra (data,mask) operand pairs for parts 1..UF-1.
  - Updated codegen for ExtractLastActive to fold parts in order, using select in scalar VF and llvm.experimental.vector.extract.last.active in vector VF.
  - Added VPWidenPHIRecipe clone robustness for null underlying PHIs (cast_if_present), relevant to synthetic phis introduced by FindLast handling.

2. Formal Semantic Modeling
- Before fix (effective behavior):
  - ExtractLastActive was fixed-arity and only represented one data/mask pair.
  - Interleaving for FindLast was effectively blocked in user-visible flow (late processLoop gate), so multi-part aggregation semantics were unavailable in this path.
- After fix:
  - For default D and part pairs (V0,M0), (V1,M1), ... (Vk,Mk), result is:
    R0 = D
    Ri+1 = ELA(Vi, Mi, Ri)   (vector VF)
    or Ri+1 = select(Mi, Vi, Ri) (scalar VF)
    final = Rk+1
  - This models “concatenate parts in increasing part order, choose last active lane globally; if none active, return D”.
- Numerical example (scalar VF, UF=4):
  - D = -1
  - Part values/masks: (10,false), (20,true), (30,false), (40,true)
  - Expected: last active part is part3 => 40.
  - Pre-fix fixed-arity cannot represent all parts; after fix chain yields: select(false,10,-1)=-1; select(true,20,-1)=20; select(false,30,20)=20; select(true,40,20)=40.

3. Assumptions and Preconditions Identified
- ExtractLastActive operands must be well-formed: 1 default + 2*N trailing operands as (data,mask) pairs.
- Per-pair data/mask types must match expected scalar/vector mode and each other across parts.
- Unroll must append pairs in correct part order to preserve “last active” semantics.
- The find-last transform introduces ExtractLastActive before unroll in a form that matcher/unroller recognizes.
- Synthetic VPWidenPHIRecipe instances may have null underlying IR PHI and cloning must tolerate it.

4. Potential Cases to Break Assumptions
- UF>2 or odd UF (e.g., 3) where ordering bugs are invisible at UF=2.
- Cases with active masks in multiple parts where wrong fold direction changes winner.
- Remainder/epilogue paths where vector middle result must merge with scalar continuation.
- Verifier-enabled pipelines where synthetic widened PHI cloning can assert/crash if null-underlying handling is incomplete.
- Cases with no active masks at all, requiring strict preservation of default value across all parts.

5. Test Strategies
- Focus on mutating existing find-last/conditional-assignment tests to force IC>2, especially IC=3 and IC=4.
- Validate both scalar VF (select-chain lowering) and vector VF (intrinsic-chain lowering).
- Include dynamic trip-count remainder cases to stress vector-to-scalar handoff.
- Include -vplan-verify-each in at least one stress test to catch structural/clone issues early.

{"found": false, "tool": "difftest", "action": "test", "original_ir": "
target triple = "aarch64-linux-gnu"

@arr0 = global [8 x i32] [i32 1, i32 2, i32 3, i32 100, i32 5, i32 6, i32 7, i32 8], align 16

define i32 @simple_csa_int_select_use_interleave(i64 %N, ptr %data, i32 %a) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %data.phi = phi i32 [ -1, %entry ], [ %select.data, %loop ]
  %ld.addr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %data, i64 %iv
  %ld = load i32, ptr %ld.addr, align 4
  %select.cmp = icmp slt i32 %a, %ld
  %select.data = select i1 %select.cmp, i32 %ld, i32 %data.phi
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %exit.cmp, label %exit, label %loop

exit:
  ret i32 %select.data
}

define i32 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call i32 @simple_csa_int_select_use_interleave(i64 8, ptr getelementptr inbounds ([8 x i32], ptr @arr0, i64 0, i64 0), i32 15)
  ret i32 %r
}
